text.title: AWS Architecture Services Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules every application of `AWS Architecture Services` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Use Lambda for variable / spiky traffic, execution < 15min, cold-start tolerance, no GPU. Use containers (ECS Fargate / EKS) for steady ≥ 1M req/day, GPU, persistent websockets, K8s ecosystem. Document the boundary in an ADR.
rule.rationale: A binary choice is wrong for most teams; the hybrid boundary is the actual decision.

rule.statement: Use EventBridge for cross-service decoupled routing, Step Functions for stateful orchestration ≥ 2 sequential steps, SQS for async buffering + retry, SNS for fan-out. Mixing roles (SQS for routing, SNS for state) is forbidden.

rationale.source: AWS Application Integration docs; SaaS architecture postmortems

rule.rationale: Service mis-selection creates invisible retry loops + observability holes.

rule.statement: Every architecture review MUST cover all six Well-Architected pillars (Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, Sustainability). Skipping any pillar is rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Skipping a pillar creates technical debt: missing Security → breaches; missing Cost → uncontrolled spend; missing Sustainability → audit gap.

rule.statement: Default to Graviton (ARM64) compute for all new resources; require an explicit comment to justify x86. Audit Graviton coverage quarterly.
rule.rationale: Graviton delivers ~20% lower cost + comparable / better perf for most workloads; x86 default is unjustified waste.

rule.statement: EKS clusters MUST be deployed in private subnets, IRSA enabled, secrets encryption via KMS, managed node groups on Graviton, and CoreDNS / kube-proxy / VPC CNI / EBS CSI as managed add-ons.
rule.rationale: Public-subnet workers + IAM-keys-on-nodes is the most common EKS misconfiguration class.

rule.statement: Aurora production deployments MUST run Multi-AZ (≥ 2 instances), KMS-encrypted at rest, IAM authentication enabled, deletion protection on, retention ≥ 30 days, Performance Insights + Enhanced Monitoring (60s) on.

rationale.source: AWS Aurora Best Practices; SOC2 audit findings

rule.rationale: Single-AZ Aurora has no automatic failover; deletion-protection-off has cost teams entire databases.

rule.statement: ALB MUST redirect HTTP → HTTPS, use SSL policy `ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06` minimum, and ship access logs to S3 with ≥ 90d lifecycle.
rule.rationale: Default ALB security policies allow weak ciphers; TLS 1.3 policy is the defensible baseline.

rule.statement: Every EventBridge rule target MUST have a DLQ + retry policy configured; use a custom event bus for application events with 30-day event-archive retention.

rationale.source: AWS EventBridge ops guide; event-driven postmortems

rule.rationale: Without DLQ, EventBridge silently drops events after 24h of retries; archive enables replay during outages.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
